instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertions on Why the Premium is Lower in a Group Term Policy Than in an Individual Term Policy- Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: group term life insurance, term policy, term insurance plan, group insurance policy for employees
Body Content:
Introduction
There is no doubt that there have been many advancements in the society we live in, and these correspond to the standard of living we enjoy. While these are positive changes that come with evolving technology, changes also mean that your daily cost of living sees increases. Additionally, healthcare expenses have hit the roof, and meeting these is a challenge for most people. However, there are ways and means that you can provide your family and loved ones with some degree of financial security. To do this, it is sensible to go in for a term policy, that is, avail of something called a term insurance plan. This kind of insurance gives your near and dear ones enough financial coverage, should they ever have a requirement for this in the future.
Know About Plans and Premiums
Whether you opt for individual term life insurance or get the benefit of group term life insurance, you should always be aware of what your premium values are for either of these. For instance, in the case of individual term life insurance plans, individuals can get coverage according to the premium that has been paid. Additionally, the policy stands valid for a particular duration as opted for by an individual. In case of the insured individual’s unfortunate demise during the tenure of the policy, the insurer pays a death benefit to the nominee, or the beneficiary named in the policy document. The payment received is either a lump sum amount or a staggered amount (given across many months for a set period), based on the terms of the product chosen. Several factors must be accounted for while opting for any term insurance plan. Certain information regarding the gender, age, lifestyle habits, medical case history, present income, the estimated retirement time, etc, is considered while deciding premiums.
To know more about what affects premiums (which may be high or low) of term policies, it is important to know about the policies themselves. Now that you have understood that an individual term policy provides coverage in an individual's name, you should also know a bit about group term life insurance. This is a kind of term life insurance by way of one policy being issued to cover a group of people. Typically, you will discover that organisations opt for such term life insurance policies for their staff and employees. In such a case the employer is assigned the contract, or policy, and then coverage is offered as a benefit to employees. Here, the premium paid is one amount, and it is done to cover the group as a whole.
What Affects the Insurance Premium?
When you know the difference between a group insurance policy for employees and a term insurance plan for individuals, you will grasp how premiums are affected in different ways.  The value of premiums of group term insurance policies can amount to 20% to 30% lower values when compared with premiums of individual term policies. Moreover, this is prevalent in spite of similar coverage. Specifically, in terms of a group term policy, the premium contains three components that affect premium value when you compare group term insurance with individual term insurance. They are explained here:
The Risk Premium - In a group term life insurance policy, the premium for risk is lower as it is equally spread over a group. Furthermore, this group (employees of any given company) tends to be homogenous as every individual is involved in occupations that are similar.
The Investment Component - There is no investment component for both kinds of term plans, so this does not affect premiums of either kinds of insurance plans.
Office Expense Loading - This is lower in a group term plan as there is a single policy covering the whole group.
A Case on Lower Group Insurance Premiums
It's easy to make a case for lower insurance premiums found with a term policy for a group, as the following case study highlights:
Mr. Ramesh is employed at a famous multinational in Chennai. As such, he is entitled to several benefit schemes that are offered by his employer. This includes a term life insurance cover amounting to Rs. 1 cr. Considering the fact that Mr. Ramesh has a growing family, and has just become the father of a second child, a son, he has taken a decision to purchase a private, individuals term insurance policy for himself. He has done this on the advice of experts such as professional advisors and financial planners.
Mr. Ramesh has received quotes from insurance companies. He finds that all these are significantly higher than for the identical amount that is offered to him by his employer. Expecting premium values to be the same, if not lower, Mr. Ramesh is shocked to find that individual term insurance premiums are approximately 30% higher than the benefit available from his employer.
On further scrutiny, his own financial advisor revealed why premiums of individual policies may be high. Besides other variables, the advisor also suggested that Mr. Ramesh’s employer may be enjoying the benefit of some claim-free years. Therefore, the premium may be discounted with no-claim bonuses. In contrast to group term life insurance, any individual term policy will work out costlier as the risk premium is higher. In terms of age and occupation, Mr. Ramesh had been quoted Rs. 8,000 for the group term life insurance plan offered by his employer, and Rs. 14,000 for the individual term plan.
Conclusion
By all accounts, the times we live in are unpredictable. As a result of such uncertainty, any forms of security that you can avail of are of prime importance. If you have sufficient financial backing, you can ensure that the future of loved ones is taken care of in your absence. A term policy offers you this, and is one ideal way to be stress-free. You can avail of some good plans provided you are ready to pay the premiums due. When you apply for such plans, the younger you are, the lesser the premium to be paid is. A term insurance electronic calculator can help you in computing the premiums to be paid with any term plan.
Sources:
https://securenow.in/insuropedia/premiums-lower-group-term-policy-individual-term-policies/
Individual vs Group Term Life Insurance | Bajaj Allianz Life
